text:
HEMATOLOGY / BONE MARROW TRANSPLANT OUTPATIENT CLINIC
ROUTINE FOLLOW-UP VISIT — 18 MONTHS POST-ALLOGENEIC HEMATOPOIETIC STEM CELL TRANSPLANT

ATTENDING: Hematology/BMT Attending
FELLOW: Hematology/Oncology Fellow

---

CHIEF COMPLAINT:
Routine 18-month post-transplant surveillance visit. Patient presents for scheduled follow-up following matched unrelated donor (MUD) allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplant (alloHSCT) for AML with myelodysplasia-related changes (AML-MRC), FLT3-ITD high allelic ratio, ELN adverse-risk disease. No acute complaints.

---

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Ms. Margaret Okonkwo is a 49-year-old Nigerian-American woman and licensed practical nurse who presents today for her 18-month post-alloHSCT surveillance evaluation. She was diagnosed approximately 22 months ago with AML-MRC arising from antecedent MDS, presenting with a WBC of 84,000/uL, hemoglobin of 7.1 g/dL, and severe thrombocytopenia (platelets 28,000/uL), with 62% blasts on bone marrow biopsy and FLT3-ITD mutation identified at high allelic ratio (VAR 0.82). NGS at diagnosis additionally revealed co-mutations in DNMT3A p.R882H, ASXL1 p.G646Wfs*12, SRSF2 p.P95H, and RUNX1 p.R204Q — a mutational landscape conferring ELN 2022 adverse-risk classification and strongly favoring consolidative alloSCT in CR1.

She received standard 7+3 induction chemotherapy with cytarabine 200 mg/m2 continuous infusion over days 1-7 and daunorubicin 60 mg/m2 on days 1 through 3, with midostaurin 50 mg BID added on days 8-21 per the RATIFY protocol given her FLT3-ITD mutation. Her induction course was complicated by E. coli bacteremia with febrile neutropenia (treated successfully with 14 days of cefepime), grade 3 mucositis requiring TPN support, and transfusion dependence, all of which resolved. Day +30 bone marrow biopsy confirmed morphologic complete remission (CR) with MRD negativity by 8-color flow cytometry and undetectable FLT3-ITD by PCR.

She received one cycle of high-dose cytarabine (HiDAC) consolidation at 3 g/m2 q12h on days 1, 3, and 5, which was complicated by grade 2 cerebellar toxicity — ataxia and dysmetria — developing on day 6, requiring early discontinuation after 5 of 6 planned doses. Neurotoxicity resolved fully without long-term sequelae.

Approximately 4 months after diagnosis, she underwent a 10/10 HLA-matched unrelated donor alloHSCT (PBSC, CD34+ dose 5.8 x 10^6/kg) using reduced-intensity conditioning with fludarabine/busulfan. GVHD prophylaxis consisted of tacrolimus, mini-methotrexate (days +1, +3, +6, +11), and rabbit ATG. Neutrophil engraftment occurred at day +16, and day +30 bone marrow biopsy confirmed full donor chimerism (98% whole marrow, 99% myeloid) with MRD negativity.

Post-transplant course was notable for CMV reactivation at day +45 (peak PCR 1,840 IU/mL), treated pre-emptively with valganciclovir 900 mg BID with clearance by day +60, without end-organ disease. She also developed mild chronic GVHD at approximately day +90 with skin lichenoid changes (~15% BSA), oral mucosal involvement (lichenoid changes, xerostomia), and borderline dry eye symptoms (Schirmer's 7 mm). NIH global severity was mild (score 1). This was managed topically with clobetasol cream, oral dexamethasone rinse, and cyclosporine eye drops, without the need for systemic corticosteroids.

At the 12-month anniversary evaluation, she was doing very well — chronic GVHD was improving, tacrolimus was being tapered, and bone marrow biopsy confirmed ongoing MRD-negative remission with full donor chimerism. CT chest/abdomen/pelvis at 18 months was recently performed and showed no evidence of disease relapse or extramedullary AML. She had successfully completed her tacrolimus taper and was fully off all immunosuppression by approximately month 15 post-transplant.

Today, she reports no fevers, chills, or night sweats. She notes mild persistent dry mouth, which she manages with saliva substitutes and pilocarpine. She returned to full-time nursing work approximately 2 months ago and tolerates her work schedule without difficulty. She denies bleeding, bruising, or new lymphadenopathy. No respiratory symptoms, no GI complaints, no joint stiffness or skin changes suggesting recrudescent GVHD. She endorses mild fatigue at end of workday, which she attributes to her demanding schedule. No bone pain. No headaches or visual changes. Libido somewhat reduced (she acknowledges this but declines hormonal evaluation at this time).

She takes metformin 1000 mg BID (type 2 DM, HbA1c 6.8% at last check, well-controlled) and lisinopril 10 mg daily (hypertension, well-controlled at home). She resumed all post-transplant vaccinations per protocol. She notes excellent compliance with saliva substitutes, pilocarpine, and artificial tear drops.

---

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
- Constitutional: Mild end-of-day fatigue, no fevers, no night sweats, no unintentional weight loss (weight stable).
- HEENT: Persistent mild xerostomia (saliva substitute/pilocarpine use ongoing); mild dry eye symptoms stable on artificial tears; no oral ulcers; no new lichenoid plaques; no visual changes.
- Pulmonary: No dyspnea, no cough, no wheezing. No pleuritic chest pain.
- Cardiovascular: No palpitations, no chest pain, no lower extremity edema.
- GI: Normal appetite and oral intake. No nausea, vomiting, abdominal pain, or diarrhea. No melena or hematochezia. Bowel habits normal.
- GU: Normal urinary function. No dysuria. No hematuria.
- Musculoskeletal: No joint pain, stiffness, or swelling. No myalgias. No difficulty with gait.
- Skin: No new rash, no skin tightening, no erythema. Prior lichenoid skin changes on forearms/dorsal hands essentially resolved.
- Neurological: No headache, numbness, tingling, or weakness. No cognitive changes. No balance problems (prior HiDAC-related cerebellar toxicity fully resolved).
- Hematologic: No easy bruising or unusual bleeding.
- Endocrine: Type 2 DM well-controlled. Occasional hot flushes (possibly premature ovarian insufficiency in context of conditioning regimen — not yet fully evaluated).
- Psychiatric: Mood good. Has resumed social activities and full-time work. No depression or anxiety per self-report.

---

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY:
1. AML with myelodysplasia-related changes (AML-MRC), FLT3-ITD high allelic ratio, ELN adverse-risk — diagnosed age 48; in MRD-negative CR1 post-alloHSCT.
2. Myelodysplastic syndrome (MDS) with refractory cytopenia with multilineage dysplasia (RCMD) — antecedent, observed without treatment for ~18 months prior to AML transformation.
3. Hypertension — managed with lisinopril.
4. Type 2 diabetes mellitus — managed with metformin.
5. Chronic GVHD, mild (skin, oral, ocular) — resolved by NIH criteria, no active treatment required beyond supportive measures.
6. Prior E. coli bacteremia (induction hospitalization) — resolved.
7. Prior CMV reactivation post-alloHSCT (day +45) — resolved, no end-organ disease.
8. Prior cytarabine-related cerebellar toxicity (grade 2, HiDAC cycle 1) — fully resolved.

SOCIAL HISTORY:
Ms. Okonkwo is a 49-year-old woman of Nigerian descent who immigrated to the United States approximately 20 years ago. She lives with her husband and two adult children. She is a licensed practical nurse and has returned to full-time employment. She is a lifelong never-smoker. Alcohol use: rare (occasional glass of wine at family gatherings). No illicit drug use. Strong social support system; family very involved in her care. She is active in her church community. Financially insured through employer.

FAMILY HISTORY:
Mother: hypertension, type 2 diabetes mellitus. Father: deceased from prostate cancer at age 70. One sibling: healthy (not HLA-matched for donation). No known family history of hematologic malignancy or hereditary cancer syndrome.

ALLERGIES:
No known drug allergies (NKDA).

MEDICATIONS:
1. Metformin 1000 mg PO BID (type 2 diabetes mellitus)
2. Lisinopril 10 mg PO daily (hypertension)
3. Pilocarpine 5 mg PO TID (chronic GVHD-related xerostomia)
4. Artificial tear drops (carboxymethylcellulose) 1-2 drops each eye 4x daily (dry eye)
5. Saliva substitute spray (Biotene) PRN oral dryness
6. Calcium 600 mg + Vitamin D 1000 IU PO daily (bone health post-transplant)
7. Trimethoprim-sulfamethoxazole (Bactrim DS) 1 tablet PO three times weekly (PCP prophylaxis — currently being evaluated for discontinuation per protocol)
8. Acyclovir 400 mg PO BID (HSV/VZV prophylaxis — per post-transplant protocol, reassessing duration)

Recent vaccinations (per post-transplant schedule):
- Inactivated influenza vaccine: administered this season
- Pneumococcal conjugate (PCV15) series: completed
- Tdap booster: administered
- Hepatitis B series: in progress
- COVID-19 booster: up to date

---

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
General: Well-appearing, well-nourished African American woman in no acute distress. Alert and oriented x3. Appears stated age. Cheerful affect.
Vital Signs: Blood pressure 124/78 mmHg, heart rate 76 bpm and regular, respiratory rate 14 breaths/min, temperature 36.7°C (98.1°F), oxygen saturation 98% on room air, weight 72 kg (stable from last visit), height 162 cm, BMI 27.4 kg/m2.
HEENT: Normocephalic and atraumatic. Oropharynx: mild residual xerostomia noted clinically; no active ulcerations; no lichenoid plaques or erythematous patches on buccal mucosa or gingiva. Dentition appears intact. Sclera: anicteric. Conjunctivae: mildly dry appearance bilaterally but non-injected. No pallor of conjunctivae. No icterus. No cervical, submandibular, or supraclavicular lymphadenopathy.
Skin: No rash. No erythema or hyperpigmentation. Forearms and dorsal hands: minimal residual post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation from prior lichenoid changes; no active plaques; no skin thickening or sclerosis. No new skin lesions. No ecchymoses or petechiae.
Chest/Pulmonary: Chest expansion symmetric. Breath sounds clear to auscultation bilaterally; no wheezing, rales, or rhonchi. No dullness to percussion.
Cardiovascular: Regular rate and rhythm. S1 and S2 normal without murmurs, rubs, or gallops. No peripheral edema. Peripheral pulses 2+ bilaterally.
Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. No hepatomegaly. Spleen not palpable (consistent with resolved splenomegaly on recent imaging). No masses. Bowel sounds active in all four quadrants. No ascites.
Musculoskeletal: Full range of motion of all joints. No joint effusions. Gait normal; no ataxia. No bony tenderness on palpation of spine, hips, or long bones.
Neurological: Alert, oriented x3. Cranial nerves II-XII intact. Motor strength 5/5 in all extremities. Sensation intact to light touch. Coordination: finger-nose-finger intact, heel-shin intact. Reflexes 2+ and symmetric. No evidence of peripheral neuropathy.
Lymph Nodes: No lymphadenopathy in cervical, supraclavicular, axillary, or inguinal regions.

---

LABORATORY RESULTS:
Complete Blood Count:
- WBC: 6,200/uL (reference 4,500-11,000/uL)
- Hemoglobin: 13.4 g/dL (reference 12.0-16.0 g/dL)
- Hematocrit: 39.8%
- MCV: 89 fL (normocytic)
- Platelets: 234,000/uL (reference 150,000-400,000/uL)
- ANC: 3,800/uL — adequate neutrophil recovery
- Lymphocytes: 1,680/uL
- Monocytes: 420/uL
- Eosinophils: 180/uL
- Basophils: 22/uL
Comment: CBC entirely within normal limits with full count recovery. No cytopenias. Peripheral smear: no circulating blasts, no dysplastic forms, no abnormal lymphocytes.

Comprehensive Metabolic Panel:
- Sodium: 138 mEq/L, Potassium: 4.1 mEq/L, Chloride: 102 mEq/L, Bicarbonate: 24 mEq/L
- BUN: 14 mg/dL, Creatinine: 0.82 mg/dL, eGFR: >60 mL/min/1.73m2
- Glucose: 106 mg/dL (fasting; patient reports she ate this morning)
- Total protein: 7.1 g/dL, Albumin: 4.2 g/dL
- Total bilirubin: 0.6 mg/dL, Direct bilirubin: 0.2 mg/dL
- ALT: 22 U/L, AST: 19 U/L, Alkaline phosphatase: 64 U/L
- LDH: 168 U/L (within normal limits; previously elevated at 890 U/L at AML diagnosis)
- Uric acid: 4.3 mg/dL
Comment: Normal hepatic and renal function. LDH within normal limits — reassuring for absence of active disease.

Immunologic/Infectious:
- CMV PCR: NEGATIVE (undetectable)
- EBV PCR: NEGATIVE
- Tacrolimus level: not applicable (patient is off all immunosuppression)

Endocrine/Metabolic:
- HbA1c: 6.8% (well-controlled type 2 DM; consistent with prior measurement)
- TSH: 2.1 mIU/L (normal)
- FSH: 78.4 mIU/mL (markedly elevated; consistent with premature ovarian insufficiency/primary ovarian failure, likely from conditioning regimen — addressed below)
- Estradiol: 12 pg/mL (low; consistent with ovarian insufficiency)

Immunologic/Lymphocyte Reconstitution (flow cytometry lymphocyte subset panel):
- CD4+ T-cells: 380/uL (recovering; prior nadir post-transplant was <50/uL)
- CD8+ T-cells: 540/uL
- CD4/CD8 ratio: 0.7 (mildly inverted, expected at 18 months post-transplant)
- NK cells (CD56+/CD16+): 210/uL (adequate)
- B cells (CD19+): 160/uL (recovering)
Comment: Immune reconstitution proceeding appropriately. CD4 count of 380/uL above the threshold for discontinuation of PCP prophylaxis per institutional guidelines (>200/uL x 2 consecutive measurements).

Bone Marrow Biopsy Results (performed today):
- Morphology: Normocellular marrow (~40% cellularity), normal trilineage hematopoiesis, no morphologic evidence of AML. Myeloblasts <2% by morphology.
- Flow cytometry MRD: NEGATIVE (sensitivity 0.01%; no leukemia-associated immunophenotype detected)
- Donor chimerism (STR): Whole marrow 99% donor; T-cells 99% donor; Myeloid 99% donor
- FLT3-ITD digital PCR: NOT DETECTED (sensitivity <0.01%)
- DNMT3A p.R882H ddPCR: NOT DETECTED
- ASXL1 G646Wfs*12 ddPCR: NOT DETECTED
Conclusion: Sustained durable MRD-negative complete remission with full donor chimerism confirmed at 18 months post-alloHSCT. Complete molecular clearance of all previously tracked mutations.

---

IMAGING RESULTS:
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast (performed recently, reviewed at this visit):
Findings: Normal-sized spleen (9.8 cm craniocaudal, resolved from 14.2 cm at diagnosis). No hepatomegaly. No lymphadenopathy in the mediastinal, hilar, axillary, or abdominal compartments. Lungs clear bilaterally — no consolidation, no ground-glass opacities, no bronchiolitis obliterans pattern. No pleural or pericardial effusions. No lytic or blastic bony lesions. No soft tissue masses. No extramedullary disease.
Impression: No evidence of AML relapse. Resolved splenomegaly. No pulmonary or infectious complications.

---

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:

Ms. Okonkwo is a 49-year-old woman with a history of AML-MRC with FLT3-ITD high allelic ratio (ELN adverse-risk, with DNMT3A/ASXL1/SRSF2/RUNX1 co-mutations) who achieved MRD-negative complete remission following 7+3 plus midostaurin induction, underwent one cycle of HiDAC consolidation, and proceeded to a 10/10 MUD alloHSCT with RIC (flu/bu) and tacrolimus/MTX/ATG GVHD prophylaxis. She presents today at 18 months post-transplant in excellent overall condition.

1. AML-MRC, FLT3-ITD HIGH ALLELIC RATIO — IN MRD-NEGATIVE COMPLETE REMISSION, 18 MONTHS POST-ALLOhsct:
Today's bone marrow biopsy confirms ongoing MRD-negative CR with 99% full donor chimerism and complete molecular clearance of all tracked somatic mutations (FLT3-ITD, DNMT3A p.R882H, ASXL1 frameshift). Peripheral blood shows a fully recovered, normal CBC without any cytopenias. LDH is within normal range. CT chest/abdomen/pelvis recently reviewed with no evidence of extramedullary relapse or new lymphadenopathy. This is a very encouraging result at 18 months — she has now cleared the highest-risk window for relapse (the first 12-18 months post-transplant carry the greatest relapse risk for adverse-risk AML). The graft-versus-leukemia (GVL) effect is likely contributing to sustained remission. We will continue routine surveillance per BMT protocol. Next bone marrow biopsy will be performed at the 24-month time point. Next CT surveillance in 6 months.

2. CHRONIC GVHD — RESOLVED BY NIH CRITERIA:
Ms. Okonkwo's chronic GVHD has resolved by NIH consensus criteria. The skin lichenoid changes on the forearms and dorsal hands have essentially cleared, with only minimal residual post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation remaining — no active lichenoid plaques, no sclerosis. Oral mucosal involvement has resolved — no active ulcers or lichenoid changes on today's exam; mild xerostomia persists and is being managed with saliva substitutes (Biotene) and pilocarpine 5 mg TID, which she tolerates well and finds helpful. Dry eye symptoms are stable and managed adequately with cyclosporine eye drops and artificial tears. She was fully off tacrolimus by month 15 post-transplant, and she has now been off all systemic immunosuppression for approximately 3 months. No new GVHD manifestations on today's evaluation. At this point, the chronic GVHD is considered resolved and she is being managed with supportive measures only. Will continue pilocarpine and eye drops indefinitely as needed for residual sicca symptoms. She should continue to have annual ophthalmology follow-up given prior dry eye involvement.

3. IMMUNE RECONSTITUTION:
Immune reconstitution is proceeding appropriately. CD4 count is 380/uL today — above the threshold for safe discontinuation of PCP prophylaxis (Bactrim DS). Given sustained CD4 >200/uL over the past two consecutive measurements, we will discontinue Bactrim today. Acyclovir prophylaxis: per our institutional post-transplant protocol, HSV/VZV prophylaxis with acyclovir is typically continued for at least 12 months post-transplant and until immunosuppression is fully discontinued. Given that she has been off all immunosuppression for 3 months and immune reconstitution is adequate, we will discontinue acyclovir at this visit. She has completed her pneumococcal and Tdap series. She is up to date on inactivated influenza vaccine. Hepatitis B series ongoing — will check titers after series completion. We will schedule the recombinant zoster vaccine (Shingrix) series at 24 months post-transplant per standard post-transplant immunization guidelines. Live vaccines remain contraindicated until at least 24 months post-transplant with full immune reconstitution confirmed.

4. PREMATURE OVARIAN INSUFFICIENCY (POI):
New finding today: FSH markedly elevated at 78.4 mIU/mL with low estradiol (12 pg/mL), consistent with primary ovarian failure likely secondary to conditioning chemotherapy (fludarabine/busulfan) as well as prior cytarabine and anthracycline exposure. She has noted occasional hot flushes and mildly reduced libido; she had not previously mentioned these symptoms. She does not desire future fertility. We discussed the implications of POI including cardiovascular risk (premature estrogen deficiency), bone health (accelerated osteoporosis risk), and vasomotor symptom management. We will refer her to gynecology for evaluation and discussion of hormone replacement therapy (HRT). She is already taking calcium and vitamin D. DEXA scan ordered to establish baseline bone mineral density given conditioning-related and POI-related risks. This will be coordinated through her PCP as well.

5. TYPE 2 DIABETES MELLITUS:
Well-controlled. HbA1c 6.8%. She is taking metformin 1000 mg BID. Renal function normal (creatinine 0.82 mg/dL). No proteinuria noted. Continued with current regimen. Note that she had been on systemic steroids during portions of her care (though never for GVHD); endocrine function reassessed annually. Annual ophthalmology (diabetic screening) and podiatry referrals reinforced to primary care physician.

6. HYPERTENSION:
Well-controlled on lisinopril 10 mg daily. BP 124/78 mmHg today. Continued.

7. REDUCED DLCO / PULMONARY FUNCTION:
At the 12-month evaluation, PFTs showed mildly reduced DLCO at 82% predicted with normal spirometry (FEV1 88%, no obstructive pattern). She is currently asymptomatic from a pulmonary standpoint. The mild DLCO reduction is consistent with prior chemotherapy (anthracycline, cytarabine) and/or subclinical pulmonary GVHD without bronchiolitis obliterans syndrome. CT scan today shows no ground-glass opacities or obstructive features. Will repeat PFTs at 24-month evaluation.

8. QUALITY OF LIFE / FUNCTIONAL STATUS:
Ms. Okonkwo has returned to full-time nursing work approximately 2 months ago and is functioning very well. ECOG performance status 1. She reports substantially improved quality of life compared to the early post-transplant period. She notes some end-of-day fatigue consistent with her physically demanding work schedule, which we attribute to normal work exertion rather than disease-related fatigue. Psychosocially, she reports good mood, strong family support, and active participation in her church community. We acknowledged the remarkable trajectory of her recovery from an adverse-risk AML diagnosis through alloHSCT to this point of functional independence and ongoing remission. Continued supportive counseling offered; she declines formal psychological services at this time but is aware they remain available.

DISPOSITION AND FOLLOW-UP:
- Bone marrow biopsy performed today; results reviewed above — MRD-negative, full donor chimerism confirmed.
- Discontinue: Bactrim DS (PCP prophylaxis) and Acyclovir (HSV/VZV prophylaxis) effective today.
- Continue: Pilocarpine 5 mg TID, artificial tears, saliva substitutes, metformin, lisinopril, calcium/vitamin D.
- New orders: DEXA scan (osteoporosis baseline given POI and conditioning exposure); gynecology referral for POI/HRT evaluation.
- Next BMT follow-up: 24-month post-transplant visit (6 months from today). Repeat bone marrow biopsy and CT imaging at 24-month visit.
- Annual surveillance: ophthalmology follow-up for prior GVHD-related dry eye and diabetic screening; PCP involvement for diabetes, hypertension, and DEXA coordination.
- Recombinant zoster vaccine (Shingrix) series to be given at 24-month visit.
- Reminder: live vaccines remain contraindicated until confirmed at 24-month evaluation.
- Patient verbalized understanding of the plan and follow-up schedule. Questions were answered to her satisfaction. She expressed gratitude for her care team.

Cancer present: NO.
Disease status: Sustained MRD-negative complete remission (CR1), 18 months post-alloHSCT, fully off immunosuppression, chronic GVHD resolved.
